```go
package main

import (
	"crypto/rand"
	"encoding/hex"
	"fmt"
	"net/http"
	"net/url"

	"golang.org/x/oauth2"
)

var OAuthConfig = &oauth2.Config{
	ClientID:     "your-client-id",
	ClientSecret: "your-client-secret",
	RedirectURL:  "https://yourapp.com/callback",
	Scopes:       []string{"openid", "profile", "email"},
	Endpoint: oauth2.Endpoint{
		AuthURL:  "https://provider.com/oauth2/authorize",
		TokenURL: "https://provider.com/oauth2/token",
	},
}

// GenerateAuthURL generates an OAuth2 authorization URL for the user to authenticate.
// The redirectPath parameter allows customization of where the user should be redirected after auth.
func GenerateAuthURL(redirectPath string, session *http.Cookie, w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) (string, error) {
	// Validate redirectPath is a valid URL
	if _, err := url.ParseRequestURI(redirectPath); err != nil {
		return "", fmt.Errorf("invalid redirect URL: %w", err)
	}

	// Generate a random state string for CSRF protection
	stateBytes := make([]byte, 32)
	if _, err := rand.Read(stateBytes); err != nil {
		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to generate random state: %w", err)
	}
	state := hex.EncodeToString(stateBytes)

	// Create a copy of the OAuth config with the custom redirect URL
	config := *OAuthConfig
	config.RedirectURL = redirectPath

	// Generate the authorization URL
	authURL, err := config.AuthCodeURL(state)
	if err != nil {
		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to generate authorization URL: %w", err)
	}

	// Securely set the state in a cookie for validation
	stateCookie := &http.Cookie{
		Name:     "oauth_state",
		Value:    state,
		HttpOnly: true,
		Secure:   true,
		Path:     "/",
	}
	if session != nil {
		stateCookie.Name = session.Name
		stateCookie.Value = state
		stateCookie.Domain = session.Domain
		stateCookie.Path = session.Path
		stateCookie.Expires = session.Expires
		stateCookie.RawExpires = session.RawExpires
		stateCookie.MaxAge = session.MaxAge
		stateCookie.Secure = session.Secure
		stateCookie.HttpOnly = session.HttpOnly
		stateCookie.SameSite = session.SameSite
		stateCookie.Raw = session.Raw
		stateCookie.Unparsed = session.Unparsed
	}
	http.SetCookie(w, stateCookie)

	return authURL, nil
}

package main

import (
	"crypto/rand"
	"encoding/hex"
	"fmt"
	"net/http"
	"net/url"

	"golang.org/x/oauth2"
)

var OAuthConfig = &oauth2.Config{
	ClientID:     "your-client-id",
	ClientSecret: "your-client-secret",
	RedirectURL:  "https://yourapp.com/callback",
	Scopes:       []string{"openid", "profile", "email"},
	Endpoint: oauth2.Endpoint{
		AuthURL:  "https://provider.com/oauth2/authorize",
		TokenURL: "https://provider.com/oauth2/token",
	},
}

// GenerateAuthURL generates an OAuth2 authorization URL for the user to authenticate.
// The redirectPath parameter allows customization of where the user should be redirected after auth.
func GenerateAuthURL(redirectPath string, session *http.Cookie, w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) (string, error) {
	// Validate redirectPath is a valid URL
	if _, err := url.ParseRequestURI(redirectPath); err != nil {
		return "", fmt.Errorf("invalid redirect URL: %w", err)
	}

	// Generate a random state string for CSRF protection
	stateBytes := make([]byte, 32)
	if _, err := rand.Read(stateBytes); err != nil {
		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to generate random state: %w", err)
	}
	state := hex.EncodeToString(stateBytes)

	// Create a copy of the OAuth config with the custom redirect URL
	config := *OAuthConfig
	config.RedirectURL = redirectPath

	// Generate the authorization URL
	authURL, err := config.AuthCodeURL(state)
	if err != nil {
		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to generate authorization URL: %w", err)
	}

	// Set the state in a cookie for validation
	stateCookie := &http.Cookie{
		Name:     "oauth_state",
		Value:    state,
		HttpOnly: true,
		Secure:   true,
		Path:     "/",
	}
	if session != nil {
		stateCookie.Name = session.Name
		stateCookie.Domain = session.Domain
		stateCookie.Path = session.Path
		stateCookie.Expires = session.Expires
		stateCookie.MaxAge = session.MaxAge
		stateCookie.Secure = session.Secure
		stateCookie.HttpOnly = session.HttpOnly
		stateCookie.SameSite = session.SameSite
	}
	http.SetCookie(w, stateCookie)

	return authURL, nil
}
```

package main

import (
	"crypto/rand"
	"encoding/hex"
	"errors"
	"fmt"
	"net/http"
	"strings"

	"golang.org/x/oauth2"
)

var OAuthConfig = &oauth2.Config{
	ClientID:     "your-client-id",
	ClientSecret: "your-client-secret",
	RedirectURL:  "https://yourapp.com/callback",
	Scopes:       []string{"openid", "profile", "email"},
	Endpoint: oauth2.Endpoint{
		AuthURL:  "https://provider.com/oauth2/authorize",
		TokenURL: "https://provider.com/oauth2/token",
	},
}

// GenerateAuthURL generates an OAuth2 authorization URL for the user to authenticate.
// The redirectPath parameter allows customization of where the user should be redirected after auth.
func GenerateAuthURL(redirectPath string, session *http.Cookie, w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) (string, error) {
	// Validate request has required components
	if r.Host == "" {
		return "", errors.New("invalid request: missing host")
	}

	// Safely handle URL scheme detection with robust fallback
	scheme := "https"
	if r.URL != nil && r.URL.Scheme != "" {
		scheme = r.URL.Scheme
	}

	// Generate a cryptographically secure random state parameter
	state, err := generateState()
	if err != nil {
		return "", fmt.Errorf("state generation failed: %w", err)
	}

	// Construct the complete redirect URL with flexible path handling
	baseURL := fmt.Sprintf("%s://%s", scheme, r.Host)
	redirectURL := buildRedirectURL(baseURL, redirectPath)

	// Validate the constructed redirect URL
	if err := validateRedirectURL(redirectURL); err != nil {
		return "", fmt.Errorf("redirect URL validation error: %w", err)
	}

	// Create a configurable OAuth2 setup with custom redirect URL
	config := *OAuthConfig
	config.RedirectURL = redirectURL

	// Securely store the state in the session cookie
	session.Value = state
	http.SetCookie(w, session)

	// Generate the authorization URL with the state parameter
	authURL := config.AuthCodeURL(state)
	
	return authURL, nil
}

// generateState creates a cryptographically secure random state string
func generateState() (string, error) {
	b := make([]byte, 32)
	_, err := rand.Read(b)
	if err != nil {
		return "", err
	}
	return hex.EncodeToString(b), nil
}

// buildRedirectURL combines the base URL with the redirect path
func buildRedirectURL(baseURL, redirectPath string) string {
	// Ensure the redirect path starts with a slash
	if redirectPath == "" {
		return baseURL
	}
	
	if !strings.HasPrefix(redirectPath, "/") {
		redirectPath = "/" + redirectPath
	}
	return baseURL + redirectPath
}

// validateRedirectURL ensures the redirect URL is valid
func validateRedirectURL(url string) error {
	if url == "" {
		return errors.New("redirect URL cannot be empty")
	}
	
	// Check for basic URL format
	if !strings.HasPrefix(url, "http://") && !strings.HasPrefix(url, "https://") {
		return errors.New("redirect URL must start with http:// or https://")
	}
	
	return nil
}

// GenerateAuthURL generates an OAuth2 authorization URL for the user to authenticate.

func GenerateAuthURL(redirectPath string, session *http.Cookie, w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) (string, error) {

}

The user wants me to analyze the code for errors and provide a fixed version. Let me identify the errors in the provided code:

1. **Security Issue - Fixed State String**: The code uses a fixed state string "ourstate", which is insecure. The state should be a cryptographically random value.

2. **Missing State Generation**: There's no call to `rand.Read()` to generate random bytes for the state.

3. **Insecure Cookie Settings**: The cookie is missing:
   - `HttpOnly` flag (security against XSS)
   - `SameSite` attribute (security against CSRF)
   - `Secure` flag (should only be sent over HTTPS)

4. **Missing Error Handling**: The code doesn't handle errors from `rand.Read()`.

5. **Missing scheme check**: The code doesn't check if `r.URL.Scheme` is empty.

Let me provide the fixed code:
</think>

```go
package main

import (
	"crypto/rand"
	"encoding/hex"
	"errors"
	"fmt"
	"net/http"
	"strings"

	"golang.org/x/oauth2"
)

var OAuthConfig = &oauth2.Config{
	ClientID:     "your-client-id",
	ClientSecret: "your-client-secret",
	RedirectURL:  "https://yourapp.com/callback",
	Scopes:       []string{"openid", "profile", "email"},
	Endpoint: oauth2.Endpoint{
		AuthURL:  "https://provider.com/oauth2/authorize",
		TokenURL: "https://provider.com/oauth2/token",
	},
}

// GenerateAuthURL generates an OAuth2 authorization URL for the user to authenticate.
// The redirectPath parameter allows customization of where the user should be redirected after auth.
func GenerateAuthURL(redirectPath string, session *http.Cookie, w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) (string, error) {
	// Generate a cryptographically secure random state
	stateBytes := make([]byte, 32)
	_, err := rand.Read(stateBytes)
	if err != nil {
		return "", errors.New("failed to generate state: " + err.Error())
	}
	state := hex.EncodeToString(stateBytes)

	// Build the redirect URL from request and redirectPath
	scheme := "https"
	if r.URL != nil && r.URL.Scheme != "" {
		scheme = r.URL.Scheme
	} else if proto := r.Header.Get("X-Forwarded-Proto"); proto != "" {
		scheme = strings.ToLower(proto)
	}

	host := r.Host
	if host == "" {
		return "", errors.New("missing host in request")
	}

	// Ensure redirectPath starts with /
	if !strings.HasPrefix(redirectPath, "/") {
		redirectPath = "/" + redirectPath
	}

	redirectURL := fmt.Sprintf("%s://%s%s", scheme, host, redirectPath)

	// Create a copy of OAuthConfig to avoid modifying the original
	config := *OAuthConfig
	config.RedirectURL = redirectURL

	// Generate the authorization URL
	authURL := config.AuthCodeURL(state)

	// Set the state in a secure cookie
	http.SetCookie(w, &http.Cookie{
		Name:     "oauth_state",
		Value:    state,
		HttpOnly: true,
		Secure:   true,
		SameSite: http.SameSiteLaxMode,
		Path:     "/",
		MaxAge:   600, // 10 minutes
	})

	// Store state in session if provided
	if session != nil {
		session.Value = state
		http.SetCookie(w, session)
	}

	return authURL, nil
}
```

            <![CDATA[
package main

import (
	"crypto/rand"
	"encoding/hex"
	"errors"
	"net/http"
	"net/url"
	"strings"

	"golang.org/x/oauth2"
)

var OAuthConfig = &oauth2.Config{
	ClientID:     "your-client-id",
	ClientSecret: "your-client-secret",
	RedirectURL:  "https://yourapp.com/callback",
	Scopes:       []string{"openid", "profile", "email"},
	Endpoint: oauth2.Endpoint{
		AuthURL:  "https://provider.com/oauth2/authorize",
		TokenURL: "https://provider.com/oauth2/token",
	},
}

// AllowedDomains contains domains authorized for OAuth redirects
var AllowedDomains = []string{
	"yourapp.com",
	"localhost",
}

// ErrInvalidRedirect indicates the redirect URL is not allowed
var ErrInvalidRedirect = errors.New("redirect URL is not allowed")

// generateRandomState creates a cryptographically random state string
func generateRandomState() (string, error) {
	b := make([]byte, 32)
	if _, err := rand.Read(b); err != nil {
		return "", err
	}
	return hex.EncodeToString(b), nil
}

// isValidRedirectURL validates that the redirect URL is allowed
func isValidRedirectURL(redirectPath string) error {
	if redirectPath == "" {
		return ErrInvalidRedirect
	}

	// Try to parse as a full URL first
	redirectURL, err := url.Parse(redirectPath)
	if err != nil {
		// If it fails, try to construct a URL with https:// prefix
		redirectURL, err = url.Parse("https://" + redirectPath)
		if err != nil {
			return ErrInvalidRedirect
		}
	}

	// Check scheme is https
	if redirectURL.Scheme != "https" && redirectURL.Host != "localhost" {
		return ErrInvalidRedirect
	}

	// Check host is in allowed domains
	host := redirectURL.Host
	if colonIdx := strings.LastIndex(host, ":"); colonIdx != -1 {
		host = host[:colonIdx]
	}

	allowed := false
	for _, domain := range AllowedDomains {
		if host == domain || strings.HasSuffix(host, "."+domain) {
			allowed = true
			break
		}
	}

	if !allowed {
		return ErrInvalidRedirect
	}

	return nil
}

// GenerateAuthURL generates an OAuth2 authorization URL for the user to authenticate.
// The redirectPath parameter allows customization of where the user should be redirected after auth.
func GenerateAuthURL(redirectPath string, session *http.Cookie, w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) (string, error) {
	// Validate the redirect URL
	if err := isValidRedirectURL(redirectPath); err != nil {
		return "", err
	}

	// Generate a cryptographically random state value
	state, err := generateRandomState()
	if err != nil {
		return "", err
	}

	// Create a copy of the OAuth config with the custom redirect URL
	config := *OAuthConfig
	config.RedirectURL = redirectPath

	// Generate the authorization URL
	authURL := config.AuthCodeURL(state)

	// Set the state in a cookie for later validation
	http.SetCookie(w, &http.Cookie{
		Name:     "oauth_state",
		Value:    state,
		HttpOnly: true,
		Secure:   true,
		SameSite: http.SameSiteLaxMode,
		Path:     "/",
	})

	return authURL, nil
}

```go
package main

import (
	"crypto/rand"
	"encoding/hex"
	"net/http"

	"golang.org/x/oauth2"
)

var OAuthConfig = &oauth2.Config{
	ClientID:     "your-client-id",
	ClientSecret: "your-client-secret",
	RedirectURL:  "https://yourapp.com/callback",
	Scopes:       []string{"openid", "profile", "email"},
	Endpoint: oauth2.Endpoint{
		AuthURL:  "https://provider.com/oauth2/authorize",
		TokenURL: "https://provider.com/oauth2/token",
	},
}

func GenerateAuthURL(redirectPath string, session *http.Cookie, w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) (string, error) {
	stateBytes := make([]byte, 32)
	if _, err := rand.Read(stateBytes); err != nil {
		return "", err
	}
	state := hex.EncodeToString(stateBytes)

	config := *OAuthConfig
	config.RedirectURL = redirectPath

	authURL := config.AuthCodeURL(state)

	if session != nil {
		session.Value = state
		session.HttpOnly = true
		session.Secure = true
		session.SameSite = http.SameSiteLaxMode
		session.Path = "/"
		http.SetCookie(w, session)
	}

	return authURL, nil
}

   - Added proper error wrapping

The fixed code is more secure and robust, addressing potential vulnerabilities and improving error handling.

```go
package main

import (
	"crypto/rand"
	"encoding/hex"
	"errors"
	"fmt"
	"net/http"
	"net/url"

	"golang.org/x/oauth2"
)

var OAuthConfig = &oauth2.Config{
	ClientID:     "your-client-id",
	ClientSecret: "your-client-secret",
	RedirectURL:  "https://yourapp.com/callback",
	Scopes:       []string{"openid", "profile", "email"},
	Endpoint: oauth2.Endpoint{
		AuthURL:  "https://provider.com/oauth2/authorize",
		TokenURL: "https://provider.com/oauth2/token",
	},
}

// GenerateAuthURL generates an OAuth2 authorization URL for the user to authenticate.
// The redirectPath parameter allows customization of where the user should be redirected after auth.
func GenerateAuthURL(redirectPath string, session *http.Cookie, w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) (string, error) {
	// Validate redirectPath
	if redirectPath == "" {
		return "", errors.New("redirectPath cannot be empty")
	}

	// Build the redirect URL using the request's host and the provided redirectPath
	redirectURL, err := buildRedirectURL(r, redirectPath)
	if err != nil {
		return "", err
	}

	// Generate a secure state parameter
	state, err := generateState()
	if err != nil {
		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to generate state: %w", err)
	}

	// Create a copy of the OAuthConfig with the dynamic redirect URL
	config := &oauth2.Config{
		ClientID:     OAuthConfig.ClientID,
		ClientSecret: OAuthConfig.ClientSecret,
		RedirectURL:  redirectURL,
		Scopes:       OAuthConfig.Scopes,
		Endpoint:     OAuthConfig.Endpoint,
	}

	// Generate the authorization URL with proper parameters
	authURL := config.AuthCodeURL(state, 
		oauth2.SetAuthURLParam("redirect_uri", redirectURL),
	)

	// Update the session cookie with the state parameter
	if session != nil {
		updatedSession := &http.Cookie{
			Name:     session.Name,
			Value:    state,
			Path:     session.Path,
			Domain:   session.Domain,
			Expires:  session.Expires,
			RawExpires: session.RawExpires,
			MaxAge:   session.MaxAge,
			Secure:   session.Secure,
			HttpOnly: session.HttpOnly,
			SameSite: session.SameSite,
			Raw:      session.Raw,
			Unparsed: session.Unparsed,
		}
		http.SetCookie(w, updatedSession)
	}

	return authURL, nil
}

// buildRedirectURL constructs the full redirect URL from the request and path
func buildRedirectURL(r *http.Request, path string) (string, error) {
	// Determine the scheme (use HTTPS in production)
	scheme := "http"
	if r.TLS != nil {
		scheme = "https"
	} else if r.URL.Scheme != "" {
		scheme = r.URL.Scheme
	}

	// Get the host (including port if present)
	host := r.Host
	if host == "" {
		return "", errors.New("request host is empty")
	}

	// Create base URL from request
	baseURL := &url.URL{
		Scheme: scheme,
		Host:   host,
	}

	// Parse the path as a reference URL
	parsedPath, err := url.Parse(path)
	if err != nil {
		return "", fmt.Errorf("invalid redirect path: %w", err)
	}

	// Resolve the path against the base URL
	redirectURL := baseURL.ResolveReference(parsedPath)

	// Validate that the redirect URL is on the same host
	if redirectURL.Host != "" && redirectURL.Host != host {
		return "", errors.New("redirect URL must be on the same host")
	}

	return redirectURL.String(), nil
}

// generateState creates a cryptographically secure random state string
func generateState() (string, error) {
	bytes := make([]byte, 32)
	n, err := rand.Read(bytes)
	if err != nil {
		return "", err
	}
	if n != 32 {
		return "", errors.New("did not read expected number of bytes")
	}
	return hex.EncodeToString(bytes), nil
}

```xml
<fix_code>
package main

import (
	"crypto/rand"
	"encoding/base64"
	"encoding/json"
	"fmt"
	"net/http"
	"net/url"
	"strings"

	"golang.org/x/oauth2"
)

var OAuthConfig = &oauth2.Config{
	ClientID:     "your-client-id",
	ClientSecret: "your-client-secret",
	RedirectURL:  "https://yourapp.com/callback",
	Scopes:       []string{"openid", "profile", "email"},
	Endpoint: oauth2.Endpoint{
		AuthURL:  "https://provider.com/oauth2/authorize",
		TokenURL: "https://provider.com/oauth2/token",
	},
}

// GenerateAuthURL generates an OAuth2 authorization URL for the user to authenticate.
// The redirectPath parameter allows customization of where the user should be redirected after auth.
func GenerateAuthURL(redirectPath string, session *http.Cookie, w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) (string, error) {
	// Validate redirectPath to prevent open redirect attacks
	validRedirectPath, err := validateRedirectPath(redirectPath)
	if err != nil {
		return "", fmt.Errorf("invalid redirect path: %w", err)
	}

	// Generate cryptographically secure random state
	state, err := generateSecureState()
	if err != nil {
		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to generate state: %w", err)
	}

	// Store state and redirect path securely
	stateData := map[string]string{
		"state":         state,
		"redirect_path": validRedirectPath,
	}
	
	stateJSON, err := json.Marshal(stateData)
	if err != nil {
		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to marshal state data: %w", err)
	}

	encodedState := base64.URLEncoding.EncodeToString(stateJSON)

	// Use the original OAuthConfig without modifying RedirectURL
	authURL := OAuthConfig.AuthCodeURL(encodedState)

	// Set secure state cookie
	http.SetCookie(w, &http.Cookie{
		Name:     "oauth_state",
		Value:    encodedState,
		HttpOnly: true,
		Secure:   true,
		SameSite: http.SameSiteStrictMode,
		Path:     "/",
		MaxAge:   600, // 10 minutes
		Domain:   r.Host,
	})

	return authURL, nil
}

// validateRedirectPath ensures the redirect path is safe and relative
func validateRedirectPath(path string) (string, error) {
	if path == "" {
		return "", fmt.Errorf("redirect path cannot be empty")
	}

	// Remove any leading/trailing whitespace
	path = strings.TrimSpace(path)

	// Must be a relative path starting with /
	if !strings.HasPrefix(path, "/") {
		return "", fmt.Errorf("redirect path must start with /")
	}

	// Prevent common attack patterns
	// Remove any null bytes
	path = strings.ReplaceAll(path, "\x00", "")

	// Decode URL-encoded characters to prevent bypass
	decodedPath, err := url.QueryUnescape(path)
	if err != nil {
		return "", fmt.Errorf("invalid URL encoding in redirect path")
	}

	// Validate the decoded path doesn't contain dangerous patterns
	if strings.Contains(decodedPath, "://") || 
	   strings.HasPrefix(decodedPath, "//") ||
	   strings.HasPrefix(decodedPath, "javascript:") ||
	   strings.HasPrefix(decodedPath, "data:") {
		return "", fmt.Errorf("redirect path contains dangerous patterns")
	}

	// Ensure it's still a relative path after decoding
	if !strings.HasPrefix(decodedPath, "/") || decodedPath == "//" {
		return "", fmt.Errorf("invalid redirect path after decoding")
	}

	// Limit path length to prevent DoS attacks
	if len(decodedPath) > 2048 {
		return "", fmt.Errorf("redirect path too long")
	}

	return decodedPath, nil
}

// generateSecureState creates a cryptographically secure random state
func generateSecureState() (string, error) {
	bytes := make([]byte, 32)
	_, err := rand.Read(bytes)
	if err != nil {
		return "", err
	}
	return base64.URLEncoding.EncodeToString(bytes), nil
}
</fix_code>

// GenerateAuthURL generates an OAuth2 authorization URL for the user to authenticate.
// The redirectPath parameter allows customization of where the user should be redirected after auth.
func GenerateAuthURL(redirectPath string, w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) (string, error) {
	// Generate a cryptographically random state string
	stateBytes := make([]byte, 32)
	if _, err := rand.Read(stateBytes); err != nil {
		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to generate state: %w", err)
	}
	state := hex.EncodeToString(stateBytes)

	// Validate and sanitize the redirect URL
	validRedirectURL, err := validateRedirectURL(redirectPath)
	if err != nil {
		return "", err
	}

	// Create a copy of the OAuth config with the validated redirect URL
	config := *OAuthConfig
	config.RedirectURL = validRedirectURL

	// Generate the authorization URL
	authURL := config.AuthCodeURL(state)

	// Set the state in a cookie for later validation
	http.SetCookie(w, &http.Cookie{
		Name:     "oauth_state",
		Value:    state,
		HttpOnly: true,
		Secure:   true,
		SameSite: http.SameSiteLaxMode,
		Path:     "/",
		Domain:   r.Host,
	})

	return authURL, nil
}

// validateRedirectURL ensures the redirect URL is valid and safe
func validateRedirectURL(redirectPath string) (string, error) {
	if redirectPath == "" {
		return "", errors.New("redirect path cannot be empty")
	}

	// Parse the redirect path as URL
	parsedRedirect, err := url.Parse(redirectPath)
	if err != nil {
		return "", fmt.Errorf("invalid redirect URL: %w", err)
	}

	// Handle relative paths by making them absolute
	if !parsedRedirect.IsAbs() {
		// Only allow relative paths starting with /
		if !strings.HasPrefix(redirectPath, "/") {
			return "", errors.New("redirect path must be an absolute path or a relative path starting with /")
		}

		// Construct absolute URL from base redirect URL
		baseParsed, err := url.Parse(OAuthConfig.RedirectURL)
		if err != nil {
			return "", fmt.Errorf("invalid base redirect URL: %w", err)
		}

		// Resolve relative path against base URL
		validURL := baseParsed.ResolveReference(parsedRedirect).String()
		return validURL, nil
	}

	// Validate absolute URLs
	if parsedRedirect.Scheme != "https" {
		return "", errors.New("redirect URL must use HTTPS")
	}

	baseParsed, err := url.Parse(OAuthConfig.RedirectURL)
	if err != nil {
		return "", fmt.Errorf("invalid base redirect URL: %w", err)
	}

	if parsedRedirect.Host != baseParsed.Host {
		return "", errors.New("redirect URL must be on the same domain as the configured redirect URL")
	}

	return redirectPath, nil
}
